title: Pan Roasted Syrian Potato Roast
description: A traditional Syrian dish featuring roasted russet potatoes in a briny and molten sauce, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This recipe is onion and garlic free, making it accessible to those with dietary restrictions.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the potatoes with olive oil, salt, black pepper, sumac, and paprika until they are evenly coated.
Spread the potatoes in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast for 20 minutes.
While the potatoes are roasting, combine the lemon juice, pomegranate molasses, and water in a small saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer the sauce for 10 minutes or until it has thickened slightly.
After the potatoes have roasted for 20 minutes, remove them from the oven and brush them with the sauce.
Return the potatoes to the oven and continue to roast for an additional 20 minutes or until they are tender and caramelized.
Remove the potatoes from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the potatoes hot, garnished with additional sumac and paprika if desired.
